primary_sources.title: The Strength of Weak Ties
primary_sources.what_it_provides: structural_model/theory: foundational claim that weak ties bridge otherwise-disconnected clusters and carry non-redundant information (e.g. job leads); qualitative, non-causal.

primary_sources.title: A causal test of the strength of weak ties
primary_sources.authors: Karthik Rajkumar, Guillaume Saint-Jacques, Iavor Bojinov, Erik Brynjolfsson, Sinan Aral
primary_sources.what_it_provides: causal_estimate: randomized LinkedIn People-You-May-Know experiments varying weak-tie prevalence for >20M users over 5 years (~2B new ties, ~600k new jobs); establishes a CAUSAL, nonlinear (inverted-U) effect of tie strength on job mobility.

formal_model: Hazard/probability that a social tie transmits a job (or opportunity) is a nonlinear, concave (inverted-U) function of tie strength s: P(transmit | tie) rises as ties get weaker but only up to a point, then declines (diminishing returns to weakness). Which s maximizes transmission is measurement-dependent: moderately weak ties by mutual-connection count vs the weakest ties by interaction-intensity. Effect sign is moderated by industry digitalness.
causal_assumptions: Rajkumar 2022 identifies the causal effect via algorithmic randomization of recommended ties (PYMK A/B variation) — exogenous variation in tie formation, not observational tie choice.
Interpreting it as a general 'opportunity diffusion' law assumes transportability from LinkedIn/job-search to other opportunity types (unwarranted without evidence).
Granovetter alone supplies NO causal identification (theoretical/associational).
required_variables: tie_strength (mutual-connection count AND interaction intensity — they diverge)

reported_coefficients.name: tie_strength_to_job_transmission_shape
reported_coefficients.context: Weaker ties raise job transmission up to a point, then diminishing marginal returns to tie weakness.

reported_coefficients.name: which_ties_maximize_mobility
reported_coefficients.value_or_null: moderately weak ties (by mutual connections); weakest ties (by interaction intensity)
reported_coefficients.context: Measurement-dependent result; weak ties help most in more-digital industries, strong ties in less-digital industries.

reported_coefficients.name: experiment_scale
reported_coefficients.value_or_null: >20,000,000 people; ~2,000,000,000 new ties; ~600,000 new jobs; 5 years
reported_coefficients.context: Scale of the LinkedIn field experiments (descriptive, not an effect size).

reported_coefficients.name: per_tie_job_mobility_marginal_effect_magnitude
reported_coefficients.context: Point magnitude / slope of the inverted-U is figure-level in the full text; not extracted from the abstract — left null rather than invented.

datasets_in_literature: LinkedIn People-You-May-Know randomized experiments (~2015-2019); replication code+data on Zenodo (rajkumarkarthik/weak_ties_data_and_code, record 6533898)
replication_evidence: Public replication package on Zenodo (record 6533898). Single-platform result; no independent large-scale randomized replication yet.
critiques_or_nulls: Effect is industry-heterogeneous and can REVERSE (strong ties help in less-digital industries).
'Which tie is best' depends on the tie-strength operationalization (mutual connections vs interaction intensity).
External validity beyond professional-network job search is untested.
applicability_limits: Not a general information-diffusion law; specific to opportunity/job transmission.
transport_risks: high: LinkedIn-specific, algorithm-mediated tie formation, US-centric labor market
best_validation_route: Cannot locally validate the causal job-mobility claim (no local dataset pairs tie strength with labor outcomes; Higgs/Enron lack job-transmission labels). Import the inverted-U functional form as a structural prior only; magnitudes remain unverified until LinkedIn-like or labeled-mobility data is available.
planned_implementation: Register a 'weak_tie_transmission' family: opportunity-transmission hazard as an inverted-U function of tie strength, with industry-digitalness modifier. Encode only the DIRECTION from Rajkumar; leave magnitudes null/prior.
planned_parameter_packs: linkedin_direction_pack (qualitative inverted-U + industry modifier; effect magnitudes null/unverified)
promotion_blocker: No local dataset for refit; effect magnitudes not extracted (verified:false) and causal effect not transportable. Cannot exceed structural_candidate_only.

current_registry_status: partial — only the homophily-driven edge-formation term exists (inside network_rewiring); the influence-vs-homophily SEPARATION / confounding guardrail is not represented
current_executable_status: partial — homophily edge weight executable via network_rewiring; no peer-influence identification/discount mechanism implemented

primary_sources.title: Birds of a Feather: Homophily in Social Networks
primary_sources.authors: Miller McPherson, Lynn Smith-Lovin, James M. Cook
primary_sources.what_it_provides: descriptive/review: documents that homophily pervasively structures ties of every type — the reason tied nodes co-behave even absent influence.

primary_sources.title: Distinguishing influence-based contagion from homophily-driven diffusion in dynamic networks
primary_sources.authors: Sinan Aral, Lev Muchnik, Arun Sundararajan
primary_sources.what_it_provides: causal_estimate (bounded): dynamic matched-sample estimation on a large IM/mobile-service adoption network shows homophily explains >=50% of apparent contagion and naive methods overstate influence by 300-700% — an UPPER BOUND on influence, not point identification.

primary_sources.title: Homophily and Contagion Are Generically Confounded in Observational Social Network Studies
primary_sources.authors: Cosma Rohilla Shalizi, Andrew C. Thomas
primary_sources.what_it_provides: methodological: proves homophily, contagion, and covariate causation are GENERICALLY CONFOUNDED in observational network data; separation requires randomization or strong, unverifiable parametric/covariate assumptions.

formal_model: Observed co-behavior among tied nodes = peer_influence + latent_homophily (tie formation on shared traits) + external/contextual confounds. From observational tie + behavior data this decomposition is NOT identified (Shalizi-Thomas). Best achievable without randomization: an UPPER BOUND on influence via dynamic matching on time-varying covariates (Aral): influence_share <= ~0.498, homophily_share >= ~0.50.
causal_assumptions: CENTRAL CAVEAT: observational tie/co-behavior data does NOT identify peer influence — homophily and contagion are generically confounded (Shalizi-Thomas 2011).
Aral's matched-sample estimate identifies only an UPPER BOUND under selection-on-observables (dynamic propensity matching on measured time-varying covariates); latent homophily remains uncontrolled.
Point identification of influence requires randomization of exposure (which this family, on observational data, lacks).
required_variables: rich dynamic covariates (for any matching attempt)

reported_coefficients.name: homophily_share_of_perceived_contagion
reported_coefficients.context: Adoption of a paid mobile service application on a global instant-messaging network; dynamic matched-sample vs naive estimation.

reported_coefficients.name: overestimation_of_peer_influence_by_naive_methods
reported_coefficients.uncertainty_or_null: up to 7x for users with >=4 adopter friends
reported_coefficients.context: Random matching vs dynamic matched sample; quantifies homophily-induced inflation of contagion estimates.

reported_coefficients.name: identifiability_of_influence_from_observational_ties
reported_coefficients.context: Formal result: absent randomization or strong assumptions, influence cannot be separated from homophily/covariate causation.

datasets_in_literature: Aral 2009: global instant-messaging network + adoption of a paid mobile service application (exact n not extracted here)
McPherson 2001: review synthesizing many homophily studies
Shalizi-Thomas 2011: methodological (reanalysis/critique, incl. Christakis-Fowler obesity contagion)
replication_evidence: Aral is a single large-scale observational study with matched-sample robustness checks. The Shalizi-Thomas confounding result is a general theorem, repeatedly invoked to discount observational contagion claims (no counter-result).
critiques_or_nulls: Shalizi-Thomas 2011: even dynamic matching relies on unverifiable selection-on-observables; latent homophily can never be fully controlled — strongest null against any observational influence point estimate.
Any peer-effect coefficient learned from Higgs/Enron (observational) is an upper bound at best and likely homophily-inflated.
applicability_limits: Applies as a GUARDRAIL wherever the world model would infer peer influence from observational network co-behavior.
Not a source of a transportable influence coefficient.
transport_risks: high: the influence/homophily split is specific to the covariate set and context of estimation
local_refit_feasible: partial — Enron/Higgs can DEMONSTRATE the confounding (naive contagion estimate >> homophily-adjusted estimate) and refit the homophily edge term, but CANNOT identify a true causal influence coefficient (no randomized exposure locally).
best_validation_route: Use Enron (temporal email, attributes) and Higgs (follower graph + diffusion) to reproduce the naive-vs-adjusted contagion gap as a methodological demonstration; adopt Aral's >=50% homophily share and 3-7x overestimation as a prior/guardrail that discounts observational peer-effect estimates. Do NOT promote any local influence coefficient to causal.
expected_applicability: high as an identification guardrail; low as a point estimator of influence
planned_implementation: (a) Keep homophily edge-formation term in network_rewiring. (b) Add an 'influence_identification_guard': any peer-effect estimated from observational data is flagged upper-bound and discounted by the Aral homophily share (>=0.5). (c) Optionally refit homophily edge weights on Enron.
planned_parameter_packs: aral_homophily_share_prior (>=0.50 of apparent contagion attributable to homophily; naive-method inflation 3x-7x)
promotion_blocker: Causal peer-influence is not identifiable from local observational data; the family can reach locally_validated only for the confounding-DEMONSTRATION / homophily-edge part, never transfer_validated for an influence coefficient.

family: Network targeting / seeding (friendship-nomination targeting; friendship paradox)
current_executable_status: none (a diffusion family exists separately and could host seeding, but no targeting/seed-selection mechanism is registered)

primary_sources.title: Social network targeting to maximise population behaviour change: a cluster randomised controlled trial
primary_sources.authors: David A. Kim, Alison R. Hwong, Derek Stafford, D. Alex Hughes, A. James O'Malley, James H. Fowler, Nicholas A. Christakis
primary_sources.what_it_provides: causal_estimate: cluster RCT (32 rural-Honduras villages, 5,773 participants; NCT01672580) randomizing seed-TARGETING method; friendship-nomination seeding causally beat random and in-degree seeding for health-behavior adoption.

primary_sources.title: Why Your Friends Have More Friends Than You Do
primary_sources.what_it_provides: structural_model: the friendship paradox — a randomly nominated friend has, in expectation, higher degree than a randomly chosen individual; the mathematical basis for why nomination targeting reaches more-central seeds without measuring the network.

formal_model: Seed-set selection rule maps to expected cascade size. Nomination targeting: sample a random individual, seed a randomly named FRIEND of theirs; by the friendship paradox E[degree(nominated friend)] > E[degree(random node)], so seeds are systematically more central, enlarging diffusion. Contrast rules: random seeding; in-degree (highest-degree) seeding. Coupled to a diffusion model, cascade_size(nomination) > cascade_size(random) ~= cascade_size(in-degree) in the Kim RCT.
causal_assumptions: Kim 2015 identifies the targeting-method effect via village-level randomization (clusters assigned to nomination / in-degree / random) — causal for the TARGETING RULE's effect on population adoption, assuming negligible between-village spillover.
The friendship paradox itself is a deterministic property of any degree-heterogeneous graph (no assumptions).
Transporting absolute effect sizes to other populations/behaviors is not licensed.
required_variables: node degree (for in-degree comparison and paradox check)

reported_coefficients.name: nomination_vs_random_multivitamin_adoption
reported_coefficients.context: Nutritional-supplement redemption: nomination 74.3% (951/1280) vs random 66.2% (940/1420); all pairwise differences p<0.01.

reported_coefficients.name: indegree_targeting_vs_random
reported_coefficients.context: In-degree (most-connected) seeding = 61.0% (744/1220); targeting highest-degree individuals was NOT better than random — key null.

reported_coefficients.name: study_scale
reported_coefficients.context: Rural Honduras; interventions = chlorine (water) + multivitamins; ClinicalTrials NCT01672580.

reported_coefficients.name: friendship_paradox
reported_coefficients.context: Mathematical guarantee on any degree-heterogeneous graph; explains why nomination targeting reaches more-central seeds.

datasets_in_literature: Kim 2015: 32-village Honduras cluster RCT (NCT01672580), chlorine + multivitamin adoption
replication_evidence: Single large field RCT (Kim 2015) for the field effect; friendship paradox is analytically guaranteed and universally reproducible on any graph.
critiques_or_nulls: In-degree targeting failed to beat random — naive centrality targeting is not sufficient (must exploit nomination, not raw degree).
Effect sizes are behavior- and context-specific (rural offline health products).
applicability_limits: Offline community health-behavior diffusion; digital-platform seeding dynamics may differ.
Requires nomination elicitation or a proxy for it.
transport_risks: high: rural-Honduras population and specific health products; absolute +12.2pp unlikely to transport
the RANKING (nomination > in-degree ~= random) is more transportable than the magnitude
local_refit_feasible: partial — Higgs follower graph and Enron let you (1) verify the friendship paradox directly and (2) SIMULATE nomination vs random vs in-degree seeding on real topology with the existing diffusion family and compare cascade sizes; there is NO local randomized adoption outcome, so the field effect size cannot be validated locally.
best_validation_route: On Higgs (and Enron): (1) confirm friendship paradox (mean friend-degree > mean degree) as a cheap structural check; (2) drive the existing diffusion/exposure family with nomination vs random vs in-degree seed sets and confirm cascade-size ordering; use Kim's +12.2pp only as an external anchor, not a local target.
expected_applicability: moderate-high for ranking seeding strategies; low for absolute lift transport
planned_implementation: Register 'friendship_nomination_targeting': seed-selection operator (random-friend nomination) that composes with the diffusion family; expose expected seed-degree inflation from the friendship paradox and cascade-size comparison across rules.
planned_parameter_packs: higgs_friendship_paradox_pack (degree-inflation measured on the real follower graph)
kim2015_effect_anchor (nomination vs random +12.2pp, CI 6.9-17.9; external, not locally refit)
promotion_blocker: No local randomized adoption outcome — field effect not locally validatable. Can reach locally_validated only for the structural friendship-paradox + simulated cascade-ordering claim; magnitude stays transport-restricted.

family: Network rewiring / edge formation & decay (SAOM; triadic closure)
current_registry_status: implemented (status=implemented; executable transition + tests; NO validation history, NO parameter packs)
current_executable_status: implemented — swm.world_model_v2.registry.families.interaction:rewire_probability; simplified logistic P(edge)=sigmoid(w*(homophily-0.5)+log(age_decay)); NOT full SAOM (no reciprocity/triadic terms); defaults (decay_days=90, homophily_weight=1.0) are UNFITTED priors

primary_sources.title: Introduction to stochastic actor-based models for network dynamics
primary_sources.authors: Tom A. B. Snijders, Gerhard G. van de Bunt, Christian E. G. Steglich
primary_sources.what_it_provides: structural_model/methodological: canonical SAOM — continuous-time Markov process where actors myopically optimize a network objective (density, reciprocity, transitive triads, homophily) via tie micro-steps; parameters estimated from network panel waves (RSiena).

primary_sources.title: Empirical Analysis of an Evolving Social Network
primary_sources.what_it_provides: associational/descriptive: on a university EMAIL network (43,553 people, 1 year) tie formation is driven by closure of short cycles (triadic closure) plus shared foci/attribute similarity — directly analogous to the Enron temporal email data.

formal_model: SAOM: dN(t) as a continuous-time Markov chain; a rate function lambda_i sets when actor i gets a change opportunity, and an objective/evaluation function f_i(x) = sum_k beta_k s_ik(x) (outdegree/density, reciprocity, transitive triads/closure, homophily/covariate similarity) governs which tie change maximizes myopic utility. Repo's current reduced form keeps only homophily + tie-age decay in a logistic; full family adds reciprocity and triadic-closure terms.
causal_assumptions: SAOM parameters are MODEL-BASED: identified only if the actor-oriented generative process is correctly specified; estimated within-model from >=2 network panel waves.
Kossinets-Watts is observational/descriptive — mechanisms are associational, not interventional.
No claim of causal edge-formation effects transportable across settings; parameters are per-network.
required_variables: at least two network panel snapshots (for SAOM estimation)

reported_coefficients.name: saom_parameters_universal
reported_coefficients.context: Rate, outdegree/density, reciprocity, transitive-triads, homophily are estimated PER network panel — there are no transportable universal constants.

reported_coefficients.name: repo_rewire_defaults_unfitted
reported_coefficients.context: Current defaults are UNFITTED priors, not estimated from any dataset (verified by reading source).

reported_coefficients.name: triadic_closure_drives_tie_formation
reported_coefficients.value_or_null: qualitative (closure of short cycles + shared foci dominate edge creation)
reported_coefficients.context: University email network, 43,553 people, 1 year; associational — magnitudes are dataset-specific.

datasets_in_literature: SAOM canonical panels: van de Bunt freshman networks; Glasgow 'Teenage Friends and Lifestyle' study (RSiena)
Kossinets & Watts 2006: university email network (43,553 nodes, 1 academic year)
replication_evidence: SAOM is a mature, widely-replicated method with reference software (RSiena) and many published fits. The REPO implementation, however, is a reduced logistic with no validation history and no packs.
critiques_or_nulls: Repo form omits triadic closure and reciprocity — the very mechanisms Snijders and Kossinets-Watts identify as central; it is a homophily+age-decay stub.
Repo parameters are unfitted priors (no data behind decay_days=90, homophily_weight=1.0).
SAOM requires panel data and strong generative assumptions; goodness-of-fit is sensitive to specification.
applicability_limits: Needs longitudinal edge data with timestamps; currently tagged domain=social_media_diffusion only.
Static graphs (e.g. the Higgs follower snapshot) cannot support edge-formation/decay estimation.
transport_risks: high (registry already marks transport_risk=high): SAOM parameters are setting-specific and do not transport
best_validation_route: Fit rewire_probability (age-decay + homophily) on the ENRON temporal email network (pairwise timestamped ties -> formation/decay + triadic closure); held-out route: predict future edge formation/persistence on a later time window; add triadic-closure/reciprocity terms toward SAOM and compare against Kossinets-Watts qualitative findings. (Higgs is a STATIC follower snapshot with no edge timestamps -> usable only for static degree/structure, not decay.)
planned_implementation: Extend rewire_probability with triadic-closure and reciprocity terms (move toward a lightweight SAOM evaluation function); fit on Enron; build the first parameter pack from that fit.
planned_parameter_packs: enron_rewire_pack (decay_days, homophily_weight, triadic_closure_weight FITTED on Enron temporal edges — currently unfitted/null)
promotion_blocker: Implemented but has NO validation history and NO packs; defaults are unfitted priors. Blocked from locally_validated until an Enron held-out / posterior-predictive fit record AND a parameter pack exist (per registry promotion gates).

current_executable_status: none (separate diffusion families exist; related hawkes_self_excitation is QUARANTINED after failing held-out)

primary_sources.title: COEVOLVE: A Joint Point Process Model for Information Diffusion and Network Co-evolution
primary_sources.authors: Mehrdad Farajtabar, Yichen Wang, Manuel Gomez-Rodriguez, Shuang Li, Hongyuan Zha, Le Song
primary_sources.venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 28 (NeurIPS 2015); extended in JMLR 2017
primary_sources.what_it_provides: structural_model: coupled temporal point processes where information-diffusion intensity is modulated by the network and link-creation intensity is modulated by diffusion (mutual excitation); efficient interleaved simulation; code at github.com/Networks-Learning/Coevolution.

formal_model: Two interleaved (Hawkes-like) point processes with cross terms: diffusion events (e.g. retweets) have intensity that depends on current edges; link-creation events have intensity boosted by recent diffusion exposure. Each process's conditional intensity is modulated by the other's history, yielding joint (network, cascade) traces. Parameters (base intensities, triggering kernels, diffusion<->link cross-excitation weights) fit by MLE per dataset.
causal_assumptions: Generative/fitted model, not interventional — parameters estimated by MLE assuming the specified mutually-exciting parametric form is correct.
No causal transportability claim; identification is within-model given both event streams.
required_variables: both streams jointly on the same population

reported_coefficients.name: coevolve_cross_excitation_parameters
reported_coefficients.context: Base intensities, triggering kernels, and diffusion<->link cross-excitation weights are per-dataset MLE fits; no transportable constant reported.

reported_coefficients.name: local_hawkes_self_excitation_held_out_result
reported_coefficients.value_or_null: FAILED vs Poisson (quarantined in this repo)
reported_coefficients.context: A related self-exciting point-process mechanism already failed held-out count forecasting here — a cautionary local null for coupled-Hawkes coevolution.

datasets_in_literature: COEVOLVE paper: Twitter (joint link-creation + retweet event logs) and synthetic simulations
replication_evidence: Public code (Networks-Learning/Coevolution); NeurIPS 2015 plus an extended JMLR 2017 version. No independent held-out validation in this repo.
critiques_or_nulls: Requires BOTH coupled event streams (diffusion AND link creation) on the same population — rarely available.
This repo's hawkes_self_excitation already FAILED held-out vs Poisson and is quarantined — coupled self-exciting processes risk the same overfitting here.
High parametric complexity and scalability cost.
applicability_limits: Needs joint diffusion + link-formation event logs; not satisfiable by either local dataset.
transport_risks: high: heavily parametric, platform-specific, and coupled to two event-generating processes
best_validation_route: Not locally validatable: Higgs has diffusion events but a STATIC follower graph (no link-creation timestamps -> cannot fit the network-coevolution half); Enron has communication/link events but no separate information-cascade layer. NEITHER dataset supplies both coupled streams. Given the local Hawkes held-out failure, keep gated as a structural candidate; only revisit with Twitter-style joint logs and explicit guards against the known self-excitation overfit.
planned_implementation: Defer. Register as a structural candidate only. If pursued, reuse point-process infrastructure but REQUIRE joint diffusion+link event data and a held-out check that beats the Poisson/independent-process baseline (which the repo's Hawkes did not).
promotion_blocker: No local dataset with coupled diffusion+link streams; related Hawkes self-excitation already failed held-out (quarantined). Cannot be locally validated; stays structural_candidate_only.
